key_highlights: Tech stocks rebound strongly after AI-fueled selloff, with Nasdaq 100 up over 2% as dip buyers step in.
Geopolitical tensions between US and Iran ease with halt to attacks, but oil prices rise due to Hormuz traffic slowdown.
Comcast announces spin-off of NBCUniversal and Sky, a major strategic reversal.
Yen hits 40-year low, rattling Japan and signaling potential intervention.
Gold and silver prices trend lower amid easing inflation fears and Iran tensions.
SpaceX IPO gains mostly erased, with stock down significantly from peak; analysts advise waiting for further decline.
Bitcoin and Ethereum continue to move lower, with Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) signaling potential bitcoin sale.
Supreme Court rules Trump cannot fire Fed member but grants more power over independent agencies, impacting regulatory landscape.
JPMorgan struggles to find demand for oil driller's 15% loan, indicating credit market stress in energy sector.
Two-thirds of workers secretly using banned AI apps, raising data security and compliance risks.
Medicare to start covering weight-loss drugs on July 1, a major policy shift with implications for healthcare stocks.
AI demand outstripping supply, even Google can't keep up, highlighting infrastructure bottlenecks.
Ford rehires experienced engineers after AI falls short, underscoring limitations of AI in complex manufacturing.
Voters of both parties want tighter AI regulation, signaling potential policy headwinds for tech.
China's economy picks up in June on rebounding US exports, offering a positive global demand signal.

sectors.trend: Strong rebound after selloff, with Nasdaq 100 up over 2%. AI hardware stocks create new 'MANIA' trade replacing Magnificent 7. Micron positioned as next Nvidia. Meta unveils new smart glasses. Alphabet pullback seen as opportunity. AI demand outstripping supply.
sectors.implications: Tech remains volatile but dip buyers are active. AI infrastructure and hardware stocks are in focus. Regulatory risks loom with bipartisan support for tighter AI regulation. Investors should consider hedging with leveraged ETFs on AI hardware.

sectors.trend: Oil prices rise as US and Iran halt attacks but Hormuz traffic slows. JPMorgan struggles to find demand for oil driller's 15% loan, indicating credit stress. Jet fuel prices falling but air fares not dropping. Chevron CFO explains why gas prices are stuck.
sectors.implications: Geopolitical risk premium remains but easing tensions could pressure oil. Energy sector credit markets show strain. Downstream margins may improve with falling jet fuel costs, but consumer benefit is limited. Monitor Hormuz traffic and Iran negotiations.

sectors.trend: JPMorgan sees private muni-bond accounts swell to $1.6 trillion. BofA pays $7.5 million SEC fine for reporting lapses. Supreme Court ruling on Fed independence and agency power impacts regulatory environment. Mexico central bank adds bond-buying tool.
sectors.implications: Muni-bond market growth indicates demand for tax-exempt income. Regulatory fines highlight compliance risks. Supreme Court ruling could lead to more political influence over agencies, affecting financial regulation. Mexico's move supports local bond liquidity.

sectors.trend: Medicare to cover weight-loss drugs starting July 1, a major policy shift. Eli Lilly and Regeneron named to FDA pilot program to boost US drug manufacturing. Analyst maintains Sector Perform on Regeneron.
sectors.implications: Weight-loss drug coverage expands market significantly for companies like Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly. FDA pilot program supports domestic manufacturing. Healthcare sector may see increased demand and regulatory support.

sectors.trend: Comcast spins off NBCUniversal and Sky. SpaceX IPO sparks luxury housing race in Southern California. Kraft Heinz stock treading water. 79-year-old fashion retailer closes 136 stores. Grocery sushi industry lawsuit exposes labor issues.
sectors.implications: Comcast spin-off could unlock value but creates uncertainty. SpaceX IPO-related housing demand indicates wealth creation but stock has lost gains. Consumer staples like Kraft Heinz face stagnation. Retail closures continue amid shifting consumer behavior.

sectors.trend: SpaceX IPO sparks luxury housing demand in Southern California. Mortgage rates mostly lower than refi rates. HELOC rates 61 bps lower than HEL rates.
sectors.implications: Luxury housing market in SoCal may see price appreciation from SpaceX wealth. Lower mortgage rates could support home buying, but refi activity remains subdued. HELOC rates attractive for homeowners.

sectors.trend: Bitcoin and Ethereum prices continue to move lower. Michael Saylor signals another bitcoin buy as Strategy sits $13 billion underwater. Strategy may sell up to $1.25 billion of bitcoin. Tokenization diversifies away from crypto.
sectors.implications: Crypto market remains under pressure. Strategy's potential sale could add selling pressure. Tokenization trend may shift focus from pure crypto to real-world asset tokenization. Bitcoin volatility persists.

sectors.trend: Yen hits 40-year low, rattling Japan. Gold drops on Iran tensions easing. Silver trending lower. CD rates up to 4.10% APY. High-yield savings up to 4.10% APY. 'Hawkish hold' in interest rates prompts advisors to rethink second half of 2026. NZ rate hike bets dialed back on US-Iran peace progress.
sectors.implications: Yen weakness may trigger BOJ intervention. Gold and silver decline as safe-haven demand wanes. Fixed income yields remain attractive for savers. Hawkish Fed stance suggests rates stay higher for longer. NZ rate expectations adjust lower.

key_themes: Tech rebound and AI infrastructure boom
Geopolitical tensions and oil price dynamics
Regulatory shifts: AI regulation, Supreme Court rulings, Medicare coverage
Crypto market weakness and tokenization trends
Corporate restructuring: Comcast spin-off, SpaceX IPO aftermath
Labor market and AI job displacement concerns
Consumer behavior: secret AI app usage, retirement savings challenges
Fixed income: attractive yields but hawkish Fed

risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to energy and defense stocks; increase cash or gold holdings; monitor Hormuz traffic and Iran negotiations.
Diversify tech holdings; favor companies with strong compliance and ethical AI practices; monitor bipartisan support for regulation.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Crypto market downturn and Strategy's potential bitcoin sale
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce crypto exposure; avoid leveraged crypto plays; consider tokenization plays as alternative.
Hedge USD/JPY exposure; reduce Japanese equity holdings; monitor BOJ intervention signals.
Avoid high-yield energy bonds; favor investment-grade energy companies; monitor JPMorgan loan demand as indicator.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Supreme Court ruling on agency independence
risk_assessment.mitigation: Monitor regulatory changes across sectors; expect increased political influence on agencies like SEC, FTC.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Hawkish Fed stance and interest rate uncertainty
risk_assessment.mitigation: Maintain short-duration fixed income; consider floating rate bonds; lock in CD rates at 4.10% APY.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: AI job displacement and social unrest
risk_assessment.mitigation: Invest in AI-related training and reskilling companies; monitor policy responses; avoid sectors with high automation risk.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy tech dip selectively, focusing on AI infrastructure and hardware stocks like Micron (MU) and Nvidia (NVDA).
opportunities.rationale: Nasdaq 100 rebound of 2%+ shows dip buyers are active. AI demand outstripping supply, and Micron is positioned as next Nvidia. Alphabet pullback is an opportunity.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in ETFs that hold SpaceX stock post-IPO, but wait for further price decline below $135.
opportunities.rationale: SpaceX stock has lost most of IPO gains. Analysts suggest waiting for lower entry point. ETFs provide diversified exposure.

opportunities.recommendation: Lock in high-yield savings and CD rates at 4.10% APY for cash reserves.
opportunities.rationale: Hawkish Fed stance suggests rates stay higher for longer. Fixed income yields attractive for risk-averse investors.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider healthcare stocks benefiting from Medicare weight-loss drug coverage, such as Eli Lilly (LLY) and Novo Nordisk (NVO).
opportunities.rationale: Medicare coverage starting July 1 expands market significantly. FDA pilot program supports domestic manufacturing.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy the dip in Uber (UBER) as operating metrics improve.
opportunities.rationale: Analyst report indicates depressed stock poised for comeback amid improving fundamentals.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in leveraged ETFs on AI hardware stocks to hedge bets on the 'MANIA' trade.
opportunities.rationale: New AI hardware trade replaces Magnificent 7. Leveraged ETFs can amplify returns but carry higher risk.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to energy sector high-yield bonds and stocks.
defensive_moves.rationale: JPMorgan struggling to find demand for oil driller's 15% loan signals credit stress. Geopolitical tensions add uncertainty.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge USD/JPY exposure via currency futures or ETFs.
defensive_moves.rationale: Yen at 40-year low, potential for BOJ intervention or further slide. Hedge to protect against currency risk.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce crypto holdings, especially bitcoin and ethereum.
defensive_moves.rationale: Prices continue to move lower. Strategy may sell up to $1.25 billion of bitcoin, adding selling pressure.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase allocation to short-duration Treasuries or money market funds.
defensive_moves.rationale: Hawkish Fed stance and interest rate uncertainty favor short duration. Yields remain attractive.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid stocks with high AI job displacement risk, such as retail and manufacturing.
defensive_moves.rationale: Bipartisan support for AI regulation and job displacement concerns may lead to policy headwinds. Ford rehiring engineers shows AI limitations.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Markets likely to remain volatile with a positive bias for tech, but geopolitical risks and regulatory uncertainty cap upside. Oil prices may ease if Iran tensions de-escalate further. Crypto faces continued pressure. Fixed income yields remain attractive. Yen weakness may trigger BOJ intervention, causing short-term volatility in FX markets.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: AI infrastructure buildout will drive tech earnings, but regulatory headwinds could emerge. Healthcare sector benefits from Medicare expansion. Energy sector faces credit stress and transition risks. Global economic growth supported by China rebound and US resilience. Interest rates likely to stay higher for longer, benefiting savers but pressuring growth stocks. Tokenization and digital assets may see increased institutional adoption.
key_catalysts: June jobs report (July 2, 2026) - will influence Fed rate path
Iran negotiations and Hormuz traffic normalization
Q2 earnings season, especially tech and AI companies
Supreme Court rulings on agency powers
China economic data and US export trends
watch_list: Nasdaq 100 and S&P 500 tech sector performance
Bitcoin price and Strategy's bitcoin holdings
Ford's AI vs. human engineer productivity
SpaceX stock price relative to IPO
